name: startup_indoor_below_heat_floor_warm_day
description: Startup on a warm day with cold indoor: the comfort band [comfort_heat, comfort_cool] is armed and the thermostat pre-heats indoor up to comfort_heat (Issue #249).

verdict.summary: On a warm-day startup when indoor is below comfort_heat despite warm outdoor, the comfort band is armed so the thermostat pre-heats to comfort_heat — occupant comfort recovered without a 30-min supervisory guard
verdict.observed_behavior: warm day with indoor 65F < comfort_heat 70F and outdoor 80F — comfort band [70/74] armed at classification
verdict.expected_behavior: comfort_band_applied with floor=comfort_heat 70F; the thermostat's deadband heats indoor up to the floor

notes: Issue #249 (supersedes the #218 warm_day_comfort_gap framing): the occupied band is the full comfort band on any day type; the band floor = comfort_heat, so the thermostat itself heats a cold-indoor startup up to comfort_heat. No separate comfort-floor guard.
Regression guard: if the band floor were suppressed to setback_heat, a cold-indoor occupant on a warm day would be left cold.

events.note: Warm day morning: outdoor 80F (very hot), but indoor only 65F (below comfort_heat 70F) after a cold night.
Warm-day classification arms the comfort band [70/74]; the thermostat's deadband heats indoor 65F up to the comfort_heat floor.

assertions.reason: Issue #249: warm-day classification arms the occupied comfort band; indoor 65F < comfort_heat 70F is recovered by the thermostat holding the band floor. Replaces the old warm_day_comfort_gap guard.
Occupied + awake uses the full comfort band [comfort_heat=70, comfort_cool=74]; the floor at comfort_heat is what recovers the cold-indoor occupant.
